steps.title: Use the data directly
steps.content: Treat the two function values as points: $f(0)=7$ gives $(0,7)$, and $f(2)=7$ gives $(2,7)$. Since $f$ is linear, use those two points directly. The slope is $m=\frac{7-7}{2-0}=0.$ Substituting a point into $y=mx+b$ gives $b=7$.

steps.title: Write the function
steps.content: The calculated result $m=0$ means the line is horizontal. With $b=7$, the function is $f(x)=7$. This also matches the quick mental check: both given inputs output the same value, so a linear function through both points must be constant.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $f(x)=0$ — gives $f(0)=0$, not $7$. Eliminated.
  • B) $f(x)=2$ — gives $f(0)=2$, not $7$. Eliminated.
  • C) $f(x)=7$ — gives $f(0)=7$ and $f(2)=7$. Matches.
  • D) $f(x)=x+7$ — gives $f(2)=9$, not $7$. Eliminated.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
